bead.title: checks: backfill production-reachability — internal/agent/testfixtures (6 unreached)
bead.description:
Decomposed from REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482). The Go production-reachability check (library/checks/go-production-reachability) flagged 6 symbol(s) in package `internal/agent/testfixtures` as unreachable from cli/ entry roots (deadcode RTA).

Symbols:
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:59 — ReviewFailureRunner.resultRev
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:66 — ReviewFailureRunner.failureClass
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:77 — ReviewFailureRunner.Executor
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:94 — ReviewFailureRunner.Reviewer
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:115 — ReviewFailureRunner.ReviewCalls
- internal/agent/testfixtures/review_failure_runner.go:120 — ReviewFailureRunner.ExecCalls

For each: WIRE (add to production call graph) or DELETE (genuinely obsolete). If neither is clear within ~15 min, annotate `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file a follow-up bead.

Decision rule: if the originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE; if speculative or design has moved on → DELETE.

Initial-violations evidence: .ddx/executions/20260503T124553-282667f7/initial-violations.json
bead.acceptance:
1. Each of the 6 listed symbols is either wired into the production call graph reachable from main() OR deleted (with originating bead reopened/closed-as-obsolete as appropriate).
2. Any remaining wiring:pending annotations cite open follow-up beads.
3. After landing: deadcode RTA (`go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./...` from cli/) reports zero remaining dead symbols in `internal/agent/testfixtures`.
4. cd cli && go test ./... green.
5. Decisions log written to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/decisions.md (one line per symbol: WIRE|DELETE|PENDING <reason>).
